title: Week of Mar 23-27, 2026
summary: Complete redesign of the AI Usage dashboard with efficiency metrics, anomaly detection, and overhead analysis.

features.title: AI Usage Dashboard Redesign
features.summary: Rebuilt from scratch with subscription vs API cost clarity, overhead ratio analysis, machine x agent heatmap, anomaly detection, and three focused tabs
features.description:
Complete redesign of the /ai-usage page driven by hitting the Claude Max weekly limit twice in one week.

Key improvements:
- **Summary strip** replaces 9 cards with one dense Fleet-style row showing Sub Equiv, API Spend, Tokens, Sessions, Overhead ratio, and Quota health bar
- **Anomaly detection** automatically flags high-overhead agents, daily cost spikes, and unattributed sessions
- **Machine x Agent heatmap** reveals which agents run on which machines (caught the invisible droplet usage)
- **Overhead ratio** (cache_creation / useful_tokens) on every table — the key metric for identifying workloads that should move from CLI to API
- **Efficiency tab** with per-agent overhead bars, worst offender sessions, and API cost-per-call analysis
- **Sessions tab** with filters, column chooser, and red highlighting for high-overhead sessions
- Backend: new /efficiency endpoint, quota health tracking, cost computation at ingest for $0 entries

features.title: Adaptive Quota-Aware Rate Limiting
features.summary: Automated workloads now self-regulate based on remaining weekly quota and daily budget caps
features.description:
New quota budget system prevents automated workloads from burning the weekly Claude Max subscription limit.

- **Quota Budget API** (`/ai-usage/quota-budget`): Returns real-time budget status including weekly usage vs limit, sustainable hourly rate, throttle factor, and daily automated cap. Automated scripts query this before each batch.
- **QuotaGate client** (`quota_gate.py`): 5-line integration for any workload — `gate.wait()` sleeps adaptively (1s when flush, 60s when critical) and `gate.can_proceed(cost)` stops work before hitting limits.
- **Deal enricher integration**: First consumer — enrichment batches now throttle automatically based on budget and stop gracefully when quota is exhausted.
- **Dashboard integration**: Summary strip shows Auto Budget remaining. Efficiency tab has new Budget Allocation section with weekly/daily bars, pace metrics, throttle factor, and AUTOMATED WORK BLOCKED warning.
- **60/40 budget split**: 60% of weekly budget reserved for interactive work, 40% available to automated workloads. Prevents batch jobs from locking Don out of Claude Code.
